questions.question: RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback) is used for:
options: Only game playing
Aligning LLMs (ChatGPT, Claude) to human preferences by training on human-rated responses
questions.explanation: Human labellers rate responses. A reward model is trained. PPO fine-tunes the LLM on the reward.

questions.question: Recommendation systems use RL to:
options: Only filter spam
Optimise long-term user engagement -- treating recommendation as sequential decision making
questions.explanation: YouTube, TikTok, Netflix use RL to maximise watch time or satisfaction over sessions.

questions.question: The main challenge of RL in robotics is:
options: Too simple environments
Sim-to-real gap: policies learned in simulation often fail when deployed to real hardware
questions.explanation: Physics simulators are imperfect. Domain randomisation and real-world fine-tuning help bridge the gap.

questions.question: RL in ad bidding optimises:
options: Click-through rate only
Bidding strategy across millions of auctions to maximise ROI given budget constraints
questions.explanation: Each ad impression is a state; bid is the action; revenue or clicks is the reward.

questions.question: Google's AlphaGo defeated the world champion at Go because:
options: It memorised all games
Combining deep RL (self-play) with Monte Carlo Tree Search -- too many states for pure search
questions.explanation: ~10^170 possible board positions. MCTS + policy/value networks from RL was the breakthrough.

questions.question: HVAC optimisation using RL:
options: Is not practical
Reduces data centre energy consumption by 30%+ -- Google DeepMind demonstrated this
questions.explanation: RL learns complex control policies for heating/cooling that account for thermal dynamics.

questions.question: The reward function design challenge in real-world RL:
options: Does not exist
Reward hacking: agent finds unexpected ways to maximise reward that violate the designer's intent
questions.explanation: OpenAI boat racing agent learned to spin in circles to collect power-ups instead of racing.

questions.question: Multi-agent RL (MARL) is needed when:
options: Always
Multiple agents interact -- each agent's actions affect others (games, trading, traffic control)
questions.explanation: OpenAI Five (5 Dota agents), traffic signal control, multi-robot coordination all use MARL.

questions.question: Offline RL (batch RL) learns from:
options: Real-time interaction only
A fixed dataset of historical transitions -- no environment interaction during training
questions.explanation: Important when real-world interaction is expensive or dangerous (healthcare, autonomous driving).

questions.question: The biggest practical obstacle to RL deployment vs supervised ML:
options: Lower accuracy
Sample inefficiency: RL often needs millions of environment interactions vs. a fixed labelled dataset
questions.explanation: Supervised ML can use any historical data. RL needs to actively collect data through trial and error.
